page: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The configuration includes 4 vCPUs mapped from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 102 GB of local storage. No GPU resources are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate that the D4_v3 sits in the lower performance tiers for modern compute workloads. Both single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write operations scoring in the bottom 10%, while small-block cached reads reach the bottom 25%. However, single-threaded gzip compression achieves average performance in the middle 50% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4_v3 offers a standard resource density suitable for legacy workloads or environments where high-throughput compute is not a priority. The tradeoff for its dedicated CPU allocation is lower overall processing speed and memory throughput compared to newer hardware generations. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads such as basic web serving, lightweight database operations, and development or testing environments that do not require intensive parallel processing or high-speed memory access.
description: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, delivering 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores. The instance features 16.0 GB of RAM, 102 GB of local storage, and no GPU. Benchmark data indicates lower-tier performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench CPU scores falling in the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth tests placing in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average level. Given these performance characteristics, the D4_v3 represents a legacy hardware profile with lower resource density. It is best suited for low-intensity general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and development environments where high-speed compute and memory throughput are not critical requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, delivering entry-level compute performance for basic web serving and development tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 102 GB local storage, suited for basic development workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated legacy Intel processors and local storage for entry-level workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
102 GB of local storage included with no bundled GPU accelerators
Average performance in single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks
Suited for basic web serving, lightweight databases, and development environments
